frame.mexico_role: ACS origin weights only — no scalar Mexican lifetime NPV
frame.dimensionality: education_bucket × state × descendants × MSA housing-supply elasticity
frame.rent_ledger: PUMA rent burden is incidence input; Saiz elasticity gates whether exposure ≈ renter welfare loss vs owner-transfer price discovery
frame.tension: FRBSF remittance/urban-selection channel attenuates housing demand; Saiz×ACS merge shows immigrants land in inelastic MSAs where attenuation fails

extraction_notes.saiz_dta: 269 MSAs; elasticity column read via pyreadstat. WRLURI = Wharton land-use regulatory index; S_LAND_50 / FLAT_SHARE_50_15 = topographic buildable-land shares.
extraction_notes.frbsf_html: HTML scrape; cites Albert-Monras (2022) lower housing consumption per equivalent wage and remittance-attenuated COL pass-through.
extraction_notes.vmt_pdf: Congestion/VMT externality paper — not housing supply — included as urban-per-mile local cost layer missing from NAS lifetime NPV.

parameter_claims.notes: Median 2.26; Q1 1.56; Q3 3.19. Bottom decile ≈1.07 (Miami 0.60, LA 0.63, SF 0.66). Top decile ≈4.37 (Pine Bluff 12.1).
Regulatory channel separate from topography (S_LAND_50 median 100, mean 92). Negative space: papers citing 'inelastic cities' rarely decompose regulation vs land availability.

parameter_claims.population: 237 matched MSAs, ACS 2018-22 vs Saiz quartiles
parameter_claims.notes: Q1 inelastic FB share 11.6% vs Q4 elastic 4.4%; median rent $1,343 vs $929; rent/income 20.8% vs 17.7%.

parameter_claims.population: top quintile MSAs by foreign-born share
parameter_claims.notes: 10% population shock → ~6.5% price increase vs ~3% in median MSA. Re-grades adversarial 'rent exposure ≠ welfare loss' caveat for >40% of immigrant stock.

Immigrants concentrate in high-wage/high-COL cities because remittances/origin spending reduce effective local COL pass-through; consume less housing per equivalent wage. Offset to rent-welfare-loss framing — but selection is into inelastic markets anyway.
Post-2000s inflow/return balance; urban concentration persists — fiscal lifetime studies rarely tag destination MSA elasticity quartile.

parameter_claims.population: US motorists, urban-differentiated VMT tax scenario
parameter_claims.notes: Differentiated urban/rural VMT tax raising $55B/yr highway revenue beats gasoline tax welfare by ~20% ($10.5B). Per-mile congestion channel absent from NAS housing/rent ledger.
Range −0.60 to ~0 with driver heterogeneity; urban drivers less able to adjust. Maps to immigrant urban concentration (FRBSF) as non-housing local externality.

generators.name: Rent exposure vs renter welfare loss (Saiz gate)
generators.prompt: For each fiscal/local-burden memo, tag rent findings as (a) price level, (b) renter incidence, (c) owner offset, (d) supply elasticity quartile. Where does the paper treat median gross rent as welfare loss without Saiz? Cross-walk adversarial review 'rent exposure ≠ welfare loss' to repo merge: inelastic Q1 FB 11.6% vs elastic Q4 4.4%.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced Saiz merge re-grade before using origin_puma_household_context rent burden as benign exposure; Miami/LA/SF/SJ at elasticity floor.
generators.negative_space: Lifetime NPV and PUMA rent tables omit housing supply elasticity — same rent $ can be absorbed (elastic) or pure renter loss (inelastic).
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Higher median rent in immigrant destinations equals aggregate welfare loss
Owner capital gains net out renter losses in all MSAs equally
Cross-sectional FB×rent correlation identifies immigrant-caused rent shock

generators.name: Regulatory vs topographic supply channel
generators.prompt: Decompose Saiz inelastic MSAs into WRLURI (regulation) vs S_LAND_50 / FLAT_SHARE_50_15 (topography). Which immigrant-heavy destinations are regulation-bound vs land-bound? Flag papers that cite 'build more housing' without naming which constraint binds.
generators.retrodiction: Would have split Boston/NYC/SF (low elasticity + high WRLURI) from mountain/desert MSAs; policy lever differs (zoning reform vs nothing).
generators.negative_space: Fiscal immigration debate treats housing as generic local cost — not as regulatory externality vs geographic fact.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Saiz elasticity is exogenous geography not endogenous policy
Immigrant inflow causes inelasticity rather than selecting into it
Elastic MSAs with meatpacking FB share (Sioux City, Fargo) generalize to coastal gateways
generators.theory_tested: corr(WRLURI, elasticity) vs corr(S_LAND_50, elasticity) by FB-share tercile

generators.name: Remittance-attenuated housing demand (FRBSF offset)
generators.prompt: Map FRBSF/Albert-Monras mechanism (lower housing consumption, remittance-reduced COL pass-through) against Saiz-conditional rent burden. When does urban selection into inelastic cities dominate demand attenuation? Tag origin groups by remittance intensity (World Bank) × MSA elasticity.
generators.retrodiction: Would have reconciled FRBSF 'immigrants less deterred by COL' with repo 'immigrants in inelastic expensive MSAs' — both true; net renter welfare sign needs tenure mix.
generators.negative_space: Remittance literature and rent-burden warehouse rarely joint-conditioned on elasticity quartile.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Lower housing consumption per person equals lower rent externality
Remittance outflow is fiscally neutral to US local budgets
Family vs solo migration policy (China Hukou analog) irrelevant to US fiscal cells

generators.name: Urban per-mile externality layer (VMT) missing from lifetime NPV
generators.prompt: Identify local costs that scale with urban driving not gallons: VMT/congestion (Langer-Maheshri-Winston), differentiated urban/rural tax welfare +$10.5B. Cross to FRBSF urban immigrant concentration. Does NAS lifetime ledger include any non-housing urban externality?
generators.retrodiction: Would have added per-mile congestion margin alongside PUMA rent; immigrant urban sorting → double local channel (rent + VMT) in same MSAs.
generators.negative_space: Housing-heavy vs school-heavy typology ignores third 'urban mobility' ledger line.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Immigrant urban concentration raises only housing prices
Gasoline tax incidence equals immigrant local externality
Highway trust fund transfers fully insure destination cities
generators.theory_tested: urban_immigrant_share correlated with per-mile externality exposure

generators.name: education_bucket × state × elasticity scenario cell
generators.prompt: Refuse scalar Mexico NPV. Build scenario cells: education_bucket × state × descendant_band × Saiz quartile. Which NAS cells assume national average destination when ACS weights imply inelastic-state concentration for <HS vs H-1B/high-skill buckets?
generators.retrodiction: Would have blocked pooling Mexico <HS surge cells with national rent average; CA/NY/TX gateway elasticity Q1 dominates origin_puma weights for multiple groups.
generators.negative_space: Lifetime benchmarks in npv_education_benchmarks have no destination elasticity dimension — housing incidence silently averaged out.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: NAS education cell applies to same destination mix for all origins
State average rent in warehouse equals MSA elasticity-weighted burden
Descendant housing consumption inherits parent origin remittance behavior
generators.theory_tested: NPV sign flip when attaching Q1 elasticity rent wedge to <HS cells only

theories_tested.theory: Immigrant-heavy PUMAs map to Saiz inelastic quartile — rent burden ≈ renter welfare loss
theories_tested.prediction: Weighted avg elasticity for origin_puma rows with highest rent_burden < 2.0 and FB-heavy MSAs in bottom quartile
theories_tested.duckdb_test: -- PENDING-DATA: needs an MSA-joined rent+immigrant panel (PUMA->CBSA/MSA crosswalk + rent_burden/rent_to_income + foreign_born_share + the saiz_msa_rent_immigrant_2022 elasticity*WRLURI*FB-share panel). None exist in the warehouse (exhaustive schema check 2026-06-25). Not yet runnable.
theories_tested.falsifier: High rent-burden PUMAs have median elasticity > 3.0 after MSA-PUMA crosswalk

theories_tested.theory: Regulatory index WRLURI explains elasticity variance beyond topography in immigrant gateway MSAs
theories_tested.prediction: Within top FB-share quintile, corr(elasticity, WRLURI) < corr(elasticity, S_LAND_50)
theories_tested.duckdb_test: -- PENDING-DATA: needs an MSA-joined rent+immigrant panel (PUMA->CBSA/MSA crosswalk + rent_burden/rent_to_income + foreign_born_share + the saiz_msa_rent_immigrant_2022 elasticity*WRLURI*FB-share panel). None exist in the warehouse (exhaustive schema check 2026-06-25). Not yet runnable.
theories_tested.falsifier: Topography alone explains >90% of R² in elasticity regression without WRLURI

theories_tested.theory: NAS lifetime NPV omits elasticity-conditional housing wedge — <HS benchmark understates local renter incidence
theories_tested.prediction: For npv_education_benchmarks WHERE acs_education_bucket = '<HS', attaching Q1 rent wedge (e.g. +3pp rent/income × renter share) shifts state-local ledger negative while federal row unchanged
theories_tested.duckdb_test: -- PENDING-DATA: needs an MSA-joined rent+immigrant panel (PUMA->CBSA/MSA crosswalk + rent_burden/rent_to_income + foreign_born_share + the saiz_msa_rent_immigrant_2022 elasticity*WRLURI*FB-share panel). None exist in the warehouse (exhaustive schema check 2026-06-25). Not yet runnable.
theories_tested.falsifier: Elasticity-weighted rent wedge < 5% of |individual_npv| for all <HS benchmark rows

theories_tested.theory: FRBSF remittance attenuation does not eliminate inelastic-market rent gradient
theories_tested.prediction: Even after origin remittance proxy, FB share still rises monotonically as elasticity falls across quartiles
theories_tested.duckdb_test: -- PENDING-DATA: needs an MSA-joined rent+immigrant panel (PUMA->CBSA/MSA crosswalk + rent_burden/rent_to_income + foreign_born_share + the saiz_msa_rent_immigrant_2022 elasticity*WRLURI*FB-share panel). None exist in the warehouse (exhaustive schema check 2026-06-25). Not yet runnable.
theories_tested.falsifier: Q4 elastic quartile has higher FB share than Q1 after remittance-weighting
